value.session_context: Friday close for the US cash-equity week; AP reported final index closes and weekly changes.

text: Friday, June 26, 2026 was a regular US market session for the cash-equity analysis basis used here. AP published final Friday closes for the S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Composite, and Russell 2000, and reported weekly performance through the close.

market_session: US regular cash-equity close, Friday 2026-06-26

calculation.method: AP close summary: S&P 500 7,354.02, -3.47, less than -0.1%; Dow 51,876.11, -44.51, -0.1%; Nasdaq 25,297.62, -60.99, -0.2%; Russell 2000 3,010.08, +2.23, +0.1%. Weekly and year-to-date percentages are AP-reported.

text: US equity indexes closed mixed to slightly lower on Friday, June 26, 2026. The S&P 500 slipped 3.47 points, or less than 0.1%, to 7,354.02; the Dow fell 44.51 points, or 0.1%, to 51,876.11; the Nasdaq Composite lost 60.99 points, or 0.2%, to 25,297.62; and the Russell 2000 rose 2.23 points, or 0.1%, to 3,010.08. For the week, the S&P 500 fell 2.0%, the Nasdaq fell 4.6%, the Dow rose 0.6%, and the Russell 2000 gained 1.0%.

value.sp_500_breadth: nearly two out of every three stocks within the S&P 500 rose
value.interpretation: Positive breadth was offset by high-index-weight AI and technology declines.

market_session: US regular cash-equity close, Friday 2026-06-26
text: Market breadth was better than the cap-weighted S&P 500 close implied: AP reported that nearly two out of every three S&P 500 stocks rose, even as the index slipped less than 0.1%. The divergence shows that losses in large AI-linked stocks outweighed broader gains elsewhere.

value.driver: AI valuation and funding concerns, including a New York Times report that OpenAI was considering delaying its IPO to next year.

market_session: US regular cash-equity close and Asia Friday sessions, 2026-06-26
text: AI-linked and chip shares were the main drag. AP identified Micron Technology's 6.7% drop as the heaviest weight on the market and reported ON Semiconductor down 23.7% after agreeing to buy Synaptics in an all-stock deal valued near $7 billion. AP also reported that SpaceX recovered from a 2.9% intraday drop to finish up 0.2%, while Asia's AI-linked losses included SoftBank down 12.5%, SK Hynix down 8.4%, and Samsung Electronics down 5.3%.

brent_crude.basis: AP market wrap; price described as lower than before the US and Israel attacked Iran

ten_year_treasury_yield.basis: AP market wrap, late Thursday comparison

consumer_inflation_expectations.basis: AP cited consumer inflation expectations report

market_session: US regular session close and commodity/rates late-session basis, Friday 2026-06-26
text: Oil and rates eased into the Friday close. AP reported Brent crude down 3.8% to $72.60, below its level before the US and Israel attacked Iran, and the 10-year Treasury yield down to 4.37% from 4.40% late Thursday. AP also reported one-year US consumer inflation expectations edged down to 4.6% from 4.8% in May.

market_session: US regular cash-equity session, Friday 2026-06-26
text: The session showed defensive and fuel-sensitive support underneath the headline weakness. AP reported Eli Lilly up 7.1% after an EMA committee recommendation and American Airlines up 1.7% as oil fell. CNBC reported health care was heading for a weekly gain of more than 7%, consumer staples rose more than 1% on the day, financials gained 0.8%, utilities gained 0.4%, and the S&P 500 information technology sector fell 1%.

value.kashkari_2026_rate_view: one interest rate hike by year-end
value.prior_view: one rate cut by year-end penciled in March
value.context: Kashkari linked the shift to Middle East supply risks, tariffs, fertilizer and energy disruptions, and data-center investment pressure.

market_session: Policy news context published Friday 2026-06-26
text: Fed policy risk stayed live after Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari said he had changed his outlook from one rate cut by year-end to one rate hike by year-end. CNBC reported that May headline PCE inflation rose to 4.1%, the highest since April 2023, while core PCE reached 3.4%, and that inflation has remained above the Fed's 2% goal for five years.

subject.name: AI-infrastructure and chip reversal, banks and autos pressure, lower oil

value.primary_support: renewed technology and AI-infrastructure selling reversed the prior day's Micron-led rally; banks and autos added pressure; oil weakness weighed on energy
triggers: North American chip producers reversed Thursday's Micron-guidance rally
persistent concerns over AI infrastructure costs and AI-related valuations
reports OpenAI may delay its IPO until 2027 and Apple/Microsoft may raise some product prices
lower oil as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz resumed
value.weekly_context: STOXX Europe 600 roughly unchanged to narrowly higher for the week despite Friday's decline

market_session: European regular session, Friday 2026-06-26
text: The main driver of Friday's European decline was a reversal in the AI and chip trade after Thursday's rally. Trading Economics said European stocks closed firmly lower on pressure from banks, auto producers and AI-infrastructure companies, as North American chip producers reversed their Micron-guidance rally and renewed skepticism toward the AI trade. The Germany and France pages cited chipmaker weakness, AI-infrastructure cost concerns, AI-valuation concerns, and reports that OpenAI could delay its IPO until 2027 while Apple and Microsoft considered price increases. Lower oil weighed on energy shares in London and Paris as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz resumed or more tankers left the route.

leaders.note: Irish Times/Reuters said precious-metal miners rose as gold prices increased

laggards.technology_note: Infineon, STMicroelectronics, ASML and Siemens Energy declined across the source set
laggards.energy_note: Shell and BP down over 0.9% each in London; TotalEnergies -1.3% in Paris
laggards.banks_note: Deutsche Bank -2.7%; BNP Paribas and BBVA nearly -2%; FTSE 100 also weighed by heavyweight banks

market_session: European regular session, Friday 2026-06-26
text: Friday's rotation was defensive only at the margin: precious-metal miners rose 2.5%, but technology, AI-infrastructure, banks, autos, retail, energy and chemicals were weaker. The Irish Times/Reuters wrap reported retail -1.6%, FTSE autos -3.9%, FTSE chemicals -3%, and Shell/BP down more than 0.9% each as crude fell. Trading Economics reported AI-exposed declines in Infineon, ASML, STMicroelectronics and Siemens Energy, bank weakness in Deutsche Bank, BNP Paribas and BBVA, and TotalEnergies -1.3% as oil declined.

market_session: European regular session, Friday 2026-06-26
text: Zalando was the clearest negative single-name catalyst, falling 6.3% to 6.8% after BaFin opened an investigation into the company's 2025 financial statements. Chip and AI-exposed stocks sold off: Infineon fell 4.3% to nearly 5%, STMicroelectronics fell 3.8% to 4.5%, ASML fell 1%, BE Semiconductor fell 2.2%, and Siemens Energy fell 5.6% to 5.84%. Volkswagen fell 3.7% to 3.9% on reports of potential deeper job cuts and plant closures. Gainers included Wise +9.6%, SAP +2.2%, Beiersdorf +2.1%, Danone +3.06%, Edenred +1.99% and Dassault Systemes +1.93%. Single-name values use disclosed source-specific ranges where sources differ.

oil.price_action: crude prices slid 2.3% in the Irish Times/Reuters London section; Trading Economics France cited oil down on easing supply concerns as more tankers left the Strait of Hormuz
oil.equity_effect: energy shares weighed on London and Paris; Shell and BP down over 0.9%, TotalEnergies -1.3%

gold.equity_effect: precious-metal miners rose 2.5% as gold prices increased

rates_policy.ecb_note: Trading Economics said bank shares dropped despite expectations the ECB may hold off from another rate hike this year after oil prices dropped further

market_session: European regular session, Friday 2026-06-26
text: Cross-asset signals reinforced the equity-sector split. Crude prices fell about 2.3% in the Irish Times/Reuters London section as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz resumed, and Trading Economics' France page said oil declined on easing supply concerns as more tankers left the strait. That weighed on energy shares, with Shell and BP down more than 0.9% each and TotalEnergies down 1.3%. Gold was the counterweight: precious-metal miners rose 2.5% as gold prices increased. Trading Economics also noted bank weakness despite expectations that the ECB may hold off from another rate hike this year after oil prices dropped further.

value.primary_driver: profit-taking after AI-related rallies and renewed technology valuation concerns

AP reported that shares plunged in Asia, led by heavy losses in Japan and South Korea, as traders sold to lock in gains from recent AI-related rallies. Reuters reported MSCI Asia ex-Japan fell nearly 3%. Australia was the exception, closing higher on the ASX 200 cash-market basis.

value.driver: Profit-taking after AI-related rallies, renewed Wall Street technology weakness, and concern that Apple price hikes signaled structural inflation from AI spending and limited tech components.

market_session: Asia regular cash-equity close, Friday 2026-06-26
text: The Friday Asia selloff was concentrated in AI, semiconductor, and hardware-linked stocks. Trading Economics reported SoftBank Group -12.19%, Taiyo Yuden -11.48%, Advantest -10.67%, and Tokyo Electron -4.26% in Japan; Samsung Electronics -6.28%, SK Hynix -8.95%, and SK Square -9.43% in South Korea; and SMIC -6.8% in Hong Kong. AP framed the move as profit-taking after AI rallies, while Reuters tied global pressure to Apple price hikes and concerns about AI-driven structural inflation and component scarcity.

value.ipo_context: Lingyi iTech debuted in Hong Kong with an HK$8.3 billion IPO, but the listing did not improve broader sentiment.

market_session: HKEX regular cash-equity close, Friday 2026-06-26
text: Hong Kong's Hang Seng fell 405.05 points / -1.76% to 22,671.86. Trading Economics said technology, financial, and producer manufacturing shares came under pressure as AI and semiconductor-related selling weighed on regional sentiment. Tencent fell 2.28%, AIA 1.94%, Xiaomi 3.95%, Meituan 2.80%, SMIC 6.8%, and Kingboard Laminates 5.2%. Lingyi iTech's HK$8.3 billion Hong Kong IPO did not improve broader sentiment.

tokyo_core_cpi.basis: XTB and Trading Economics context; first acceleration in eight months.

market_session: Asia session Friday 2026-06-26 with Reuters NY wrap cross-asset reference
text: Reuters reported the yen near its weakest level in 40 years at 161.76 per dollar, while the dollar index eased 0.16% to 101.35 and the euro rose 0.14% to $1.1385. US Treasury yields fell, with the 10-year at 4.38% and the 2-year at 4.096%. Tokyo core CPI accelerated to 1.6% year over year from 1.3%, and XTB reported core-core CPI rising to 1.9% from 1.6%, reinforcing expectations that the Bank of Japan could continue raising interest rates.

hormuz_shipping.basis: Reuters reported more tankers left the Strait of Hormuz and Saudi Aramco resumed oil loading at Ras Tanura after a nearly four-month halt, while a cargo vessel was hit near Oman.

value.australia_link: Trading Economics said rising copper and gold prices supported miners, with BHP +0.8%, Rio Tinto +2.2%, and Northern Star and Evolution Mining each about +3%.

market_session: Reuters NY close cross-asset reference for Friday 2026-06-26
text: The cross-asset backdrop was mixed. Reuters reported Brent crude futures down 4.34% to settle at about $72 per barrel as more tankers left the Strait of Hormuz and Saudi Aramco resumed oil loading at Ras Tanura, even though a cargo vessel was hit near Oman. Spot gold rose 1.06% to $4,068.72 per ounce. Lower oil was not enough to prevent heavy selling in Japan and Korea, but stronger gold and copper helped explain Australia's relative outperformance through miners such as BHP, Rio Tinto, Northern Star, and Evolution Mining.

market_session: BEA release 2026-06-25 08:30 EDT; market context through 2026-06-26 U.S. session
text: BEA reported that the May 2026 PCE price index rose 0.4% month over month and 4.1% from a year earlier, while core PCE rose 0.3% month over month and 3.4% year over year. CNBC described the headline annual rate as the highest since April 2023 and core as the highest since October 2023.

market_session: BEA release 2026-06-25 08:30 EDT; market context through 2026-06-26 U.S. session
text: BEA's May 2026 release showed personal income up $181.6B / 0.7%, disposable personal income up $164.9B / 0.7%, current-dollar PCE up $156.1B / 0.7%, and real PCE up $43.8B / 0.3%. The release also reported personal outlays up $159.9B, personal saving at $704.2B, and a 3.0% saving rate.

stated_drivers: energy and oil prices around the Strait of Hormuz
value.conditionality: Kashkari said the view was a pencil and data dependent

Kashkari cited supply dynamics including tariffs, fertilizer disruption, energy and oil prices around the Strait of Hormuz, and massive data-center investment, while saying the outlook remained data dependent.

Versus 06/25/2026, the 2-year fell 2 bp, the 5-year fell 3 bp, the 10-year fell 2 bp, the 20-year was unchanged, and the 30-year rose 1 bp; 2s10s stayed at +31 bp. Reuters separately reported the benchmark 10-year yield down 1.16 bp to 4.38% and the 2-year yield down 2.48 bp to 4.096%.

text: Reuters reported Brent crude futures down 4.34% to settle at $72/bbl as Hormuz supply concerns eased, USD/JPY near 161.76 and close to 40-year yen lows, the dollar index down 0.16% to 101.35 but headed for a second weekly gain, EUR/USD up 0.14% to $1.1385, and spot gold up 1.06% to $4,068.72/oz.

market_session: Final June 2026 survey release, published 2026-06-26
text: The University of Michigan final June survey showed sentiment rising to 49.5 from 44.8 in May, up 10.5% month over month but still down 18.5% from June 2025. Current conditions were 47.7 and expectations were 50.7. Year-ahead inflation expectations edged down to 4.6% from 4.8%, and long-run expectations fell to 3.3% from 3.9%.

text: Reuters reported MSCI's world-stock gauge down 0.53% and set for a 2% weekly loss, European shares down nearly 0.7% with technology down 1.17%, MSCI Asia ex-Japan down nearly 3%, South Korea's KOSPI down as much as 5.8%, and chip stocks down 5.3% on the day and set for a 7.7% weekly loss. The Dow fell 0.09%, the S&P 500 lost 0.05%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.24%, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq notching weekly losses.

market_session: regular US cash-equity session, Friday 2026-06-26 (closed)
text: Friday, June 26, 2026 was a regular US market session for the cash-equity and options analysis basis used here. AP published final Friday closes for the S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Composite, and Russell 2000, and reported weekly performance through the close. Cboe options settled normally. The session capped a volatile week in which AI-linked stocks dragged the Nasdaq to its fifth straight decline even as the broader market showed better internal breadth.

value.context: Mixed close: S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipped while Dow and Russell 2000 also finished narrowly. Nearly two out of every three S&P 500 stocks rose, but AI-linked losses kept the cap-weighted index down. Weekly split: Nasdaq -4.6%, S&P -2.0%, Dow +0.6%, Russell +1.0%.

market_session: US cash equity close, Friday 2026-06-26
text: AP reported a mixed June 26 U.S. close: the S&P 500 slipped 3.47 points, or less than 0.1%, to 7,354.02; the Dow fell 44.51 points, or 0.1%, to 51,876.11; the Nasdaq Composite lost 60.99 points, or 0.2%, to 25,297.62 (its fifth straight decline); and the Russell 2000 rose 2.23 points, or 0.1%, to 3,010.08. For the week, the S&P 500 fell 2.0%, the Nasdaq fell 4.6%, the Dow rose 0.6%, and the Russell 2000 gained 1.0%. Despite the cap-weighted decline, AP reported that nearly two out of every three S&P 500 stocks rose, showing that the losses were concentrated in large AI-linked names rather than spreading broadly.

subject.name: CNN Fear & Greed Index
subject.source_basis: CNN Fear & Greed page corroborated by Finhacker.cz CNN-tracker

value.cnn_headline_text: extreme fear is driving the US market
value.methodology_note: CNN Stock Price Breadth indicator still reflects the McClellan Summation Index methodology change noted in prior reports
value.freshness_note: CNN's 25 is a post-close June 26 snapshot (5:23 PM ET). Finhacker's 26 reflects the June 24 post-close value. The one-point CNN-Finhacker gap (25 vs 26) is a freshness artifact, not a methodological disagreement.

market_session: latest available readings at collection time after the June 26 close
text: The Fear & Greed Index deepened in fear territory on June 26. CNN's page displayed 25 (fear) with the headline text 'extreme fear is driving the US market', 'Previous close 25', '1 week ago 37', '1 month ago 60', '1 year ago 63', and 'Last updated Jun 26 at 5:23:14 PM ET'. Finhacker.cz's CNN-tracker showed 26 (fear) with a data-updated timestamp of 'Jun 24, 2026, 8:00 PM ET'. The composite slipped one point from June 25's 26 to June 26's 25 on CNN, and is down 12 points from one month ago. Four of the five visible CNN sub-indicators were labeled fear or extreme fear at the time of access: market momentum (fear), stock price strength (fear, NYSE net new 52-week highs/lows 1.47%), stock price breadth (extreme fear, McClellan Volume Summation Index at 1,408.51), and put/call options (extreme fear, 5-day average 0.69). Only market volatility was neutral (VIX below its 50-day moving average). The deepening of the put/call sub-indicator from 'fear' on June 25 to 'extreme fear' on June 26 is the notable day-over-day shift, consistent with the fifth consecutive Nasdaq decline and the persistent AI-stock overhang.

vix9d.freshness_note: June 26 close not available on Yahoo Finance at collection time; latest available is June 25

vix3m.freshness_note: June 26 close not available on Yahoo Finance at collection time; latest available is June 25

vix6m.freshness_note: June 26 close not available on Yahoo Finance at collection time; latest available is June 25

vix1y.freshness_note: June 26 close not available on Yahoo Finance at collection time; latest available is June 25

value.curve_date_basis: latest available: VIX from 2026-06-26 close; VIX9D through VIX1Y from 2026-06-25 close
value.intraday_range_note: VIX opened at 19.70, spiked to 20.72 in the morning selloff, then steadily eased to close at 18.20-low-18.41 as oil fell and yields eased into the close

nine_day_thirty_day_spread.note: VIX9D-to-VIX gap narrowed from 0.97 to 0.49 points using the latest available VIX9D; the true June 26 gap may differ if VIX9D also eased

market_session: Cboe published volatility-index data for the regular US session

calculation.method: VIX one-day percent change = (18.41 - 18.89) / 18.89 * 100 = -2.54%; intraday range 18.20-20.72; curve compares Cboe close fields for VIX (June 26) and VIX9D/VIX3M/VIX6M/VIX1Y (latest available June 25)

text: Yahoo Finance historical data showed VIX closing at 18.41 on June 26, down 0.48 points or 2.54% from 18.89 on June 25, with OHLC of open 19.70, high 20.72, low 18.20, close 18.41. Google Finance, MarketWatch, and WSJ all corroborate the 18.41 close. The intraday pattern is notable: VIX opened higher at 19.70, spiked to 20.72 during the morning AI-stock selloff, then steadily eased through the afternoon as Brent crude fell 3.8% and the 10-year Treasury yield dropped to 4.37%, closing near the session low. The Cboe volatility curve remained in contango and upward sloping: VIX at 18.41 (June 26), VIX9D at 17.92 (June 25 latest), VIX3M at 20.33 (June 25 latest), VIX6M at 22.35 (June 25 latest), and VIX1Y at 23.50 (June 25 latest). The VIX9D-to-VIX gap narrowed to approximately 0.49 points from June 25's 0.97-point spread (using latest available VIX9D). The longer tenors all eased slightly on June 25 (VIX3M -0.20%, VIX6M -0.22%, VIX1Y -1.48%), keeping the curve in orderly contango. The sharp spot-vol decline, despite the cap-weighted S&P 500's fifth straight decline, reflects the market's recognition that the selling remained concentrated in AI-linked mega-caps while breadth was improving and macro tail risks (oil, rates) eased.

value.interpretation: Two-tier pattern persists: single-stock options lean bullish (more calls than puts) while index flow leans defensive (more puts than calls). The total ratio dropped below 1.0 for the first time since June 12, but the index and SPX ratios remain above 1.0, confirming institutional tail-hedging alongside retail bullishness in single names.

market_session: Cboe end-of-day options statistics for the regular US session
text: Cboe end-of-day options statistics for June 26 showed a two-tier put/call pattern. The total put/call ratio was 0.99, with equity put/call at 0.67, index put/call at 1.18, exchange-traded products put/call at 1.14, VIX put/call at 0.57, and SPX+SPXW put/call at 1.23. Total options volume was 13,853,230 contracts (call 6,968,999 / put 6,884,231). Compared to June 25 (total 1.02, equity 0.61, index 1.16, ETP 1.38, SPX 1.31), the total ratio dropped below 1.0 for the first time since June 12, the ETP ratio fell sharply from 1.38 to 1.14, and the SPX ratio eased from 1.31 to 1.23. The pattern - bullish single-stock options flow (equity P/C 0.67, well below 1.0) alongside persistent defensive index hedging (index P/C 1.18, SPX P/C 1.23) - confirms that institutions continue buying tail protection via index products while retail positioning remains bullish on individual names. The CNN Fear & Greed 5-day average put/call ratio of 0.69 (extreme fear zone) is a different construction from the Cboe end-of-day ratios but points in the same direction: options-market sentiment is cautious overall, with the defensive bid concentrated in index products.

value.headline: Optimism Leaps
value.freshness_note: This is the latest available AAII weekly survey for the June 26 report date. The survey period closed Wednesday 6/24 and results were published Thursday 6/25. The next survey (week ending 7/1) will not be published until July 2. The survey does not capture Friday June 26 cash-session action.

market_session: latest AAII weekly survey published for the June 26 report date

calculation.method: Bull-bear spread = bullish 44.9% minus bearish 36.1% = +8.8 percentage points; prior spread = 36.6 - 39.4 = -2.8 pp; one-week swing = 8.8 - (-2.8) = +11.6 pp

text: The latest AAII sentiment survey (week ending 6/24/2026, published Thursday June 25) remains the most recent retail-investor sentiment gauge available for the June 26 report. Bullish sentiment was 44.9%, neutral 18.9%, and bearish 36.1%, leaving a bull-bear spread of +8.8 percentage points - the first above-historical-average reading in six weeks. The swing from the prior week's -2.8 pp spread was an 11.6-percentage-point one-week jump. However, the survey period closed before the June 26 cash-session action, so the bullish print does not reflect the Nasdaq's fifth straight decline or the further deepening of the Fear & Greed Index to 25. The AAII survey captured the improvement in mood driven by the Iran de-escalation, the Micron-guidance-led chip rally, and the in-line May PCE release, none of which fully offset the late-week AI-stock pressure in the composite sentiment indices.

value.data_freshness_note: ICI's latest combined-flow release (published June 24 for the week ended June 17) predates the June 23-26 AI-stock selloff; the next ICI release (week ended June 24) had not been published at collection time. ETF.com's June 22 weekly article also predates the late-week selloff. Positioning context, not real-time flow.

market_session: latest fund-flow releases available for the June 26 report date
text: The latest available fund-flow data provides positioning context heading into the June 26 session rather than a real-time flow gauge. ICI's combined-flow release (published June 24 for the week ended June 17) showed $66.23 billion of total long-term inflows, including $92.03 billion of ETF net issuance and $25.80 billion of mutual fund outflows. Equity funds took in $55.75 billion (domestic $49.60B, world $6.15B), bond funds took in $16.46 billion. ETF.com's June 22 weekly article reported $119.83 billion of total ETF inflows led by $83.62 billion into U.S. equity ETFs, with IVV (+$30.40B), SPYM (+$12.87B), XLK (+$8.33B), and SMH (+$7.44B) the top creations and SPY (-$29.62B) and VOO (-$14.42B) the top redemptions. Both data releases predate the June 23-26 AI-stock selloff, so they capture the post-SpaceX-IPO risk appetite but not the late-week de-risking. The SPY/VOO redemptions alongside IVV/SPYM creations suggest structural rotation (share-class creation flow) rather than outright equity abandonment.

value.brent_note: below its level before the US and Israel attacked Iran
value.macro_theme: Oil and yields eased (risk-positive), but Fed hawkish shift and above-4% inflation cap the upside (risk-negative). Net: classic late-week relief rally in oil/rates, not a clean risk-on regime change.

market_session: US cross-asset session close
text: AP reported Brent crude down 3.8% to $72.60, below where it stood before the US and Israel attacked Iran, and the 10-year Treasury yield eased to 4.37% from 4.40% late Thursday. AP also cited a decline in one-year US consumer inflation expectations to 4.6% from 4.8% in May. The macro relief from falling oil and yields did not remove Fed risk: CNBC reported that Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari changed his outlook from one rate cut by year-end to one rate hike by year-end, citing Middle East supply risk, tariffs, energy and fertilizer disruptions, and data-center investment pressure. CNBC also reported May headline PCE inflation at 4.1% (highest since April 2023) and core PCE at 3.4%. The cross-asset price action - Brent down sharply, yields easing, consumer inflation expectations easing - is consistent with a late-week relief bid in risk assets, but the Fed backdrop remains hawkish and inflation is still above 4%, capping the upside for sentiment.

subject.label: spot vol easing while composite fear deepens - bifurcated sentiment regime

value.cash_equity_tape: Mixed close: S&P -<0.1%, Nasdaq -0.2% (5th straight decline), Dow -0.1%, Russell +0.1%. Nearly two out of three S&P 500 stocks rose. AI-linked losses (Micron -6.7%, ON Semi -23.7%) outweighed broad gains.
value.survey_sentiment: AAII Bullish 44.9% / +8.8 pp spread (stale: published 6/25, pre-Friday action)
value.volatility: VIX -2.54% to 18.41 (intraday range 18.20-20.72); VIX9D/VIX3M/VIX6M/VIX1Y latest available June 25; curve in contango
value.options: two-tier: equity P/C 0.67 (bullish single names), index P/C 1.18, SPX P/C 1.23 (defensive hedging); total P/C below 1.0 for first time since June 12
value.flows: Latest available flows (ICI week ended June 17, ETF.com June 22) pre-date the AI-stock selloff; $83.62B into US equity ETFs in the SpaceX-IPO week
value.fear_greed: CNN 25 (fear), down from 26 on June 25 and 37 a week ago; Finhacker 26 (June 24 data); put/call sub-indicator deepened from fear to extreme fear
value.risk_read: The bifurcation between spot vol (falling sharply as oil eased) and composite sentiment (deepening to 25 as put/call and breadth indicators deteriorate) is the defining signal. The market is pricing out near-term event risk while the cumulative damage to breadth and options positioning keeps the Fear & Greed Index in the fear zone.

market_session: regular US cash-equity session synthesis from latest available sentiment indicators
text: The best synthesis for June 26 is 'spot vol easing while composite fear deepens - a bifurcated sentiment regime.' VIX dropped 2.54% to 18.41, with the intraday range (18.20-20.72) showing that the opening AI-stock panic was priced out through the afternoon as Brent crude fell 3.8% and the 10-year yield eased to 4.37%. The Cboe total put/call ratio dropped below 1.0 for the first time since June 12, and the ETP and SPX put/call ratios both eased from June 25 levels. But the Fear & Greed Index slipped further to 25 (fear), the put/call sub-indicator deepened from 'fear' to 'extreme fear' (5-day average 0.69), and stock price breadth remained in 'extreme fear' territory (McClellan Volume Summation Index at 1,408.51). The divergence between declining spot volatility and deepening composite fear is the defining signal of this cycle: the market is pricing out near-term event risk (oil easing, yields easing, VIX falling), but the cumulative damage to market breadth and options positioning from the Nasdaq's five-session decline keeps the composite sentiment index in the fear zone. The AAII survey's 44.9% bullish print is now two days stale and captures the Iran-de-escalation and chip-rally optimism rather than the Friday action. The regime is not a clean risk-on (composite sentiment is at 25, Fed is hawkish, inflation is above 4%) and not the June 23 panic (VIX fell, oil fell, breadth was positive at the stock level). It is a late-week relief bid layered over a structurally cautious positioning environment, with the institutional tail-hedge bid in index options persisting even as single-stock options flow stays bullish.
